query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "multi-turn reflective masking elicits reasoning in mask diffusion mode" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on privacy protection and PII masking in multi-turn conversations, which is a distinct construct from the user's question regarding "reflective masking" to elicit reasoning in mask diffusion models. While both involve "masking" and "multi-turn" settings, the mechanisms (privacy sanitization vs. reasoning elicitation) and objectives are entirely different, falling under the "distinct construct sharing only homonym keywords" rejection rule.
